stories.headline: Iran fires missiles at US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain after US strikes Iranian radar sites; ceasefire collapses
stories.summary: US forces shot down Iranian drones near the Strait of Hormuz early June 6 and struck Iranian coastal radar at Goruk and on Qeshm Island. Iran fired seven ballistic missiles at US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain; defenses intercepted six and one fell short. The IRGC said it forced back an oil tanker leaving Hormuz, and Iran warned of a 'complete closure' of the strait if attacks continue, shattering a fragile ceasefire.

perspectives.keyFraming: US frames the strikes as defensive responses to Iranian drone launches threatening Gulf shipping and allied bases.
perspectives.summary: US officials said American forces intercepted Iranian drones headed toward the Strait of Hormuz and then hit Iranian coastal surveillance radar at Goruk and on Qeshm Island, after which Iran launched ballistic missiles at the Ali Al Salem base in Kuwait and US facilities in Bahrain. Six of seven missiles were intercepted and one fell short, with CENTCOM calling Iranian claims of hitting the Fifth Fleet headquarters 'false.'

sources.title: U.S. military says it shot down Iranian drones launched toward Gulf allies
Iran launched missiles, drones toward Strait of Hormuz, US military says

perspectives.keyFraming: Coverage stresses the renewed exchange reopens the Hormuz oil chokepoint as a flashpoint and risks a wider Gulf war.
perspectives.summary: Reporting noted US forces struck Iranian coastal sites after Tehran sent drones toward the Strait of Hormuz, with Iran's missile salvo at US facilities in Kuwait and Bahrain marking the most serious escalation since the ceasefire extension. The IRGC said it forced back oil tankers and threatened a complete closure of the strait, raising fears of a wider Gulf war.

sources.title: US Forces Hit Iranian Coastal Sites After Tehran Launches Drones Toward Strait

perspectives.keyFraming: Trump touts a degraded Iranian arsenal and a pressure-and-strike posture that he says is working.
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning coverage centered Trump's claim that Iran retains only about 21-22% of its pre-February missile arsenal and his assertion that the US is 'moving very fast' on the conflict, with Treasury directed to assess whether seized Iranian assets could compensate Gulf allies.

sources.title: US strikes Iran radar sites after drones near Hormuz, missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain

stories.headline: Ukraine hits Russian navy base at Kronstadt in deep drone strike as SPIEF forum closes
stories.summary: Ukraine launched large-scale overnight drone strikes reaching about 1,000 km into Russia on June 6, targeting naval arsenals and bases at Kronstadt near St. Petersburg and an oil depot in Krasnodar's Ust-Labinsk. Zelensky confirmed ordering the strikes, saying 'it is time to end this war.' Russia's Defense Ministry said 376 drones were downed over 16 regions including Crimea. The strikes coincided with the close of the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum in Putin's hometown.

perspectives.keyFraming: Kyiv demonstrates growing reach to hit deep inside Russia, with pressure timed to Putin's flagship economic forum.
perspectives.summary: Western coverage reported Ukrainian drones flew roughly 1,000 km to strike naval bases and arsenals at Kronstadt and energy targets in western Russia. Zelensky said he ordered strikes on naval and energy sites and declared 'it is time to end this war,' days after Putin rejected his offer for direct talks.

sources.title: Ukraine targets Russian navy base near St. Petersburg

perspectives.keyFraming: Russia reports mass interception as the forum wraps, with competing damage claims on both sides.
perspectives.summary: Wire coverage reported Russia's Defense Ministry said 376 Ukrainian drones were downed over 16 regions including Crimea overnight, while local authorities in Krasnodar reported an oil depot at Ust-Labinsk hit and set ablaze, with the barrage coming as the SPIEF economic forum concluded.

sources.title: Russia says hundreds of Ukrainian drones downed as economic forum ends

perspectives.keyFraming: State-media framing casts the attack as a repelled Kyiv 'terror' provocation timed to SPIEF, downplaying damage.
perspectives.summary: Russian state coverage reported a large-scale Ukrainian drone attack on the St. Petersburg area, emphasizing Russian air-defense interceptions and minimizing damage while framing the strikes as provocations staged to coincide with the economic forum.

sources.title: Large-scale drone attack hits St. Petersburg

stories.headline: Nasdaq sinks ~4% in worst day since April 2025 as Broadcom's soft AI outlook triggers a chip-stock rout
stories.summary: The Nasdaq fell about 4% to 25,709, the S&P 500 dropped 2.6% and the Dow lost roughly 695 points as a semiconductor selloff wiped out hundreds of billions in market value. The slide was triggered by Broadcom, which reported 143% AI-revenue growth but guided next-quarter AI revenue below expectations, sinking its stock about 13% and dragging Nvidia, AMD, Micron and Arm lower. Hot jobs data and rising Treasury yields amplified the move.

perspectives.keyFraming: Framed as Broadcom's strong-but-not-blowout AI outlook puncturing lofty expectations and detonating a broad semiconductor selloff.
perspectives.summary: Market coverage detailed the Nasdaq's roughly 4% drop — its worst day since April 2025 — and tied it to Broadcom's guidance, with chipmakers Nvidia, AMD, Micron and Arm falling and a hot jobs report and higher yields adding pressure.

sources.title: Stock Market Today (June 5, 2026): Nasdaq falls 4% as semiconductor slide wipes $1T from markets

perspectives.keyFraming: Framed as a healthy reality check for a priced-for-perfection chip sector, not a break in the AI thesis.
perspectives.summary: Market commentary noted that even 143% AI revenue growth was not enough for a sector priced for perfection, casting the selloff as a long-overdue correction in overheated semiconductor valuations rather than a thesis-breaking event.

sources.title: Broadcom Sinks 14% on Soft AI Chip Outlook Despite Earnings Beat, Dragging Down AMD and Intel

stories.headline: Federal judge strikes down Trump policy that froze asylum and immigration benefits for 39 countries
stories.summary: US District Chief Judge John McConnell Jr. on June 5 vacated Trump administration policies that categorically barred immigrants from 39 countries from final decisions on asylum, work permits, green cards and citizenship. He said the freeze left applicants in 'indeterminate legal limbo' and was improperly driven by anti-immigration sentiment, and ordered processing to resume.

perspectives.keyFraming: A federal judge vacated sweeping Trump immigration-benefit freezes and ordered processing to resume for dozens of countries.
perspectives.summary: Coverage reported McConnell found USCIS ignored the law by indefinitely suspending asylum adjudications and freezing applications for travel-ban countries enacted after the shooting of two National Guard members. The order requires the government to restart processing for affected applicants.

sources.title: Federal judge strikes down Trump administration policy freezing immigration proceedings from 39 countries
Judge strikes down Trump policy that halted asylum decisions for 39 countries

perspectives.keyFraming: Conservatives cast the ruling as another district judge overriding national-security-driven immigration controls.
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning coverage framed the decision as judicial overreach against policies the administration adopted following the National Guard shooting, emphasizing that a single district judge forced the resumption of asylum and benefit processing for dozens of countries.

sources.title: Federal Judge Strikes Down USCIS Benefit Freeze Policies
